items.prompt: generates and tests ordered pairs for bounded linear equations in two variables
items.expected_evidence: generate ordered pairs that satisfy a bounded linear equation in two variables
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner generates or tests ordered pairs accurately against the equation.

items.prompt: explains how a linear equation in two variables determines related pairs of values and how that connects to a table or graph
items.expected_evidence: explain how the equation, table, and graph describe the same linear relationship
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the equation as a relationship among pairs rather than as a standalone expression.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to substitution_pair_breakdown.
items.expected_evidence: determine whether a given pair satisfies a linear equation
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies how the peer lost pair meaning or substitution structure.

items.prompt: succeeds across symbolic, table, verbal, graph, and simple contextual linear-equation representations
items.expected_evidence: connect slope and intercept-style structure to the behavior of the related pairs in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves two-variable linear-equation meaning across varied representations and contexts.
